name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
resistance_profile: Fixed vertical bar path removes balance demands so you can grind close to failure safely; hardest through the mid-range sticking point.
cues: Line the bench up so the bar tracks over the shoulders
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows
Control the descent, do not bounce off the bottom
execution_steps: Set an upright bench inside the Smith machine so the bar path lines up over your collarbone.
Sit down, take a slightly-wider-than-shoulder grip, and unrack by rotating the hooks off.
Lower the bar under control to chin or upper-chest height.
Press straight up along the fixed track until the elbows lock out.
Finish the set and re-rack by rotating the bar back into the hooks.
good_when: You want to push a pressing set close to failure without a spotter
Isolating the delts with minimal stabiliser fatigue
bad_when: You want free-weight stabiliser and core involvement
The fixed bar path irritates your shoulders
You are training for barbell overhead-press strength carryover
common_errors: Setting the bench too far forward so the bar pulls you
Flaring the ribs and overextending the lower back
progressions: Add a pause at the bottom stretch
regressions: Reduce load and range to a comfortable overhead position
